user_query:
I just wrapped a short planning call for my ML course study group and need you to turn it into actionable outputs and drafts I can send. Please use only the provided input files under input/ and produce all outputs under output/ (overwrite if they exist).

Inputs to use:
- input/meeting_transcript.md (source of attendees, decisions, action items, risks, dates)
- input/team_contacts.csv (source of names and emails; my manager is Mina Park)
- input/syllabus.json (source of valid module IDs)

Deliverables and constraints:
1) Structured action items (required)
- Create output/action_items.json as a JSON array where each object corresponds to exactly one ACTION line in input/meeting_transcript.md.
- Fields per item: id (AI-001, AI-002, … in order of appearance), description (concise, imperative), owner_name (must exactly match a name in team_contacts.csv), owner_email (looked up from team_contacts.csv), due_date (YYYY-MM-DD from the transcript; must parse and be on or after the meeting date), module_id (must be one of the module ids in input/syllabus.json), dependencies (array of action item ids; infer from phrases like "depends on Alice's repo skeleton" by mapping to the appropriate action; empty array if none), priority (High|Medium|Low; default to Medium only if unspecified), source_line_number (1-based line number of the ACTION line in the transcript).

2) Meeting notes (required)
- Create output/meeting_notes.md with clear sections: Title, Date, Attendees (names exactly as listed in the transcript), Summary (2–4 sentences), Decisions (bullet list from all DECISION lines), Risks (bullet list from all RISK lines), Action Items (a table with columns: id, owner_name, due_date, module_id, priority), and Next Meeting (date/time from the transcript).

3) Email to my manager (required)
- Draft an email and save it as output/email_manager.txt with:
  - To: Mina Park <email taken from team_contacts.csv>
  - Subject: Request for dedicated ML learning time + study group update
  - Body (under 200 words) that: (a) summarizes the study group outcomes in 2–3 sentences, (b) lists my commitments from output/action_items.json as a short bullet list including each action item id and due date, and (c) proposes reserving 4 hours each Friday, 13:00–17:00 PT, for the next 6 weeks starting the first Friday after the meeting date in the transcript. Keep the tone professional but friendly.

4) Study group message (required)
- Draft a short message to post in our #ml-study-group channel and save as output/study_group_message.txt (under 250 words). Include: (a) a bullet list of the key decisions, (b) a checkbox list of action items in the form "[ ] AI-00X — description (owner_name, due YYYY-MM-DD)" for every action item, and (c) a reminder of the next meeting date/time and a request for quick thumbs-up if the owners agree with their items.

5) Validation report (required)
- Create output/validation_report.md that reports:
  - total_action_items, unique_owners, modules_covered
  - For each action id: owner_in_contacts (yes/no), module_valid (yes/no), due_date_valid (yes/no and the parsed date), dependencies_valid (yes/no: all referenced ids exist), and the source_line_number echoed back.
  - Confirm that output/email_manager.txt includes the action ids for my (Jordan Lee’s) items and that all owners and module ids used match the provided inputs. If any issue is found, list it under an "Issues" section; otherwise state "No issues".

Important:
- Treat line numbers in input/meeting_transcript.md as 1-based, starting from the first line in the file.
- Do not invent attendees, decisions, actions, owners, modules, or dates that are not present in the inputs.
- All module_ids must exist in input/syllabus.json. All owner_names must exist in input/team_contacts.csv. The next Friday date for the email proposal must be computed relative to the meeting date in the transcript.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
